reasoning:
Initial analysis relies on the FRED data trend. The value on March 28 stands at a highly elevated 286.6 billion, largely due to quarter-end window dressing. While the combined statistical model implies a probability of 0.52 for the early April date to exceed this threshold, the linear baseline projection holds a lower P(exceed) at 0.38. Given that early month unwinding typically pushes RRP volumes back down, anchoring closer to the linear estimate (0.38) for April 6 is prudent. 

As we project further into 2025, continuous Quantitative Tightening is expected to systematically shrink aggregate reserves and drain the RRP facility, making it fundamentally harder to push balances above the March 28 baseline limit outside of massive window-dressing spikes. However, systemic long-term factors are naturally harder to predict precisely, which expands uncertainty. Thus, we steadily increase the probabilities to model this rising uncertainty, converging logically on 0.50 for the most distant forecast precisely exactly one year out.
